Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 20 - Pulling Bootstrap into Your Layout

It's finally time to bring in some third-party resources. In this post I'll pair Bootstrap with the Layout we built last time, so you can clearly see the difference between a layout that loaded correctly and one that didn't. I'll walk through where to drop the files, how to link them, and the shorthand tags ThinkPHP gives you for loading CSS and JS.
ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 19: Building Layouts with the Built-in Template Engine

ThinkPHP ships with its own HTML template engine, so this time we'll look at how to build a Layout with it. We'll split a page into header and footer, pull them in with the include syntax, and then level up to a proper layout file with a CONTENT placeholder. By the end you'll see that the built-in template engine is honestly good enough for most projects.
RE: Learning JS from Zero — Day 5: Arrays and Objects
RE：從零開始的學習 JS 生活
第 11 屆 iT邦幫忙鐵人賽

Day 5 of my reincarnation into JavaScript. This time we get into arrays and objects — two of the most-used data structures in JS. We'll cover array literals, reading and changing values, object literals, dot notation vs bracket notation, and why JavaScript itself is basically just one giant object.
RE: Learning JS From Zero — Day 4: Events and Event Listeners

Day 4 of my reincarnation. After getting selectors down yesterday, the next thing to learn is events and event listeners. We'll cover what an event is, how to wire up addEventListener, and the third argument nobody talks about — event bubbling vs. event capturing — plus how to stop it with stopPropagation.
ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 18 — Model Basics (4): Conditional Queries, Delete, and JSON

haha, I wandered off and now I've wandered right back. This time I want to fill in some gaps around querying — how do you run a conditional query with a Model, and how do you sort the results afterwards? We'll also cover deleting records and outputting JSON, which matters a lot now that everyone builds APIs.
HexSchool is about to launch a new service, and that service is related to what I'm currently doing. So this post is about the upcoming new service!
EP.3 Learning JS From Zero — Day 3: Grabbing HTML Elements with Selectors

Day 3 of my reincarnated life learning JS. This time we figure out how to grab HTML elements, so I'll walk through the selectors — getElementById, querySelector, getElementsByTagName, and friends — and when to reach for each one.
Day 17 - Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch: Data Operations with the Query Builder

In the last post we did all our data operations with raw SQL. This time we're switching to the tool ThinkPHP gives us — the query builder. It wraps SQL into clean, chainable methods, runs on PDO parameter binding so you're protected from SQL injection, and honestly makes CRUD way less painful. Let's go through insert, update, select, and delete the ThinkPHP way.
Re:Learning JS From Zero — Day 2: Variables, Your Must-Pick Starter Skill

Day 2 in our isekai. Before anything else, we need to learn how to create a variable — it's something you'll reach for constantly when building systems. Think of it as a must-pick starter skill on your tech tree. This post covers what variables are, how to declare them, the three basic types, and the naming rules you really don't want to break.
ThinkPHP Day 16 - Talking to the DB Without a Model

You might be wondering why we suddenly jump from basic Model operations to raw data operations. The reason is simple: I want you to see that you can also work with your data using native SQL, without going through a Model at all. In this post we'll write raw INSERT, UPDATE, SELECT, and DELETE statements through ThinkPHP's Db class, wire them up to routes, and even switch database connections on the fly. Let's take a look.
